steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The text describes Barragán's career as evolving through distinct phases, with his 1930s travels triggering a shift from his earlier style to a more modernist approach. The correct answer must reflect this three-part structure: general overview, transition trigger, and evidence of the shift.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — focuses on influence: frames the passage around who influenced Barragán rather than around the phases of his career. ✗
  • Choice B — general statement, transition, evidence: the first sentence gives the career overview, the second identifies the travel-triggered shift, and the third shows how the change appears in his work. ✓
  • Choice C — references earlier architects: introduces figures not discussed in the text, misrepresenting the focus. ✗
  • Choice D — separates early stages from transition: treats "early stages" and the transition as disconnected rather than causally linked. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
